# Pre-Merge Guard Report — 2026-04-21

**Generated by**: Pre-Merge Guard agent  
**Branches reviewed**: 3 (most recently committed, non-guard/non-report)  
**Author (all branches)**: Akenarin Kongdach

---

## Branch 1: `feat/customer-tier-phase1`

### Summary
Adds a customer tier system (GOLD / GOOD / NEW / RISKY / BLACKLIST) computed from payment history. Includes `CustomerTierService`, new `GET /customers/:id/tier` endpoint, tier badge UI, tier filter on customers list, and E2E smoke tests.

**Files changed**: 17 files, +2021 / -7 lines  
**Commits**: 11

### Issues

#### Warning (should fix before merge)

**W1 — N+1 query on list page** (`apps/api/src/modules/customers/customers.service.ts:459–468`)  
`getCustomerTier()` is called once per customer row inside `findAll()`. Each call runs 3 DB queries (customer lookup + contracts + repossession count). With default page size 50, this produces ~150 DB queries per customers-list load. Should precompute tiers in a single aggregation query or cache tier results, not fan out per row.

**W2 — Incorrect pagination total with tier filter** (`apps/api/src/modules/customers/customers.service.ts:473`)  
The tier filter is applied in-memory *after* database pagination. `customer.count({ where: { deletedAt: null } })` does not account for the tier filter, so the pagination `total` returned to the UI is always the unfiltered count. Filtering page N with tier=BLACKLIST will show fewer rows than `limit` even when more matching customers exist on later pages. Pagination is unreliable when tier filter is active.

**W3 — Type duplication** (`apps/api/src/modules/customers/dto/tier.dto.ts` vs `apps/web/src/types/customer-tier.ts`)  
`CustomerTierResponse` and `CustomerTier` are defined independently in both API and web packages. These should live in `packages/shared/` to stay in sync. If the API shape changes, the web types will silently diverge.

#### Info

**I1 — Hardcoded Tailwind amber utilities in `CustomerTierBadge.tsx`** (line 612)  
`bg-amber-500/15 text-amber-600 dark:text-amber-400 border-amber-500/30` are used directly for the GOLD tier because no semantic `gold` token exists. The other tiers correctly use `bg-success`, `bg-warning`, `bg-destructive`. This is an acceptable one-off since no standard token covers gold, but it should be noted.

**I2 — Pagination edge case**  
Requesting page 2+ with an active tier filter may return fewer rows than `limit` with no indication that more results exist — the paginator renders correctly only for page 1.

### Recommendation: **REVIEW**

W1 and W2 are not blockers for initial feature launch in a small-shop context (comment in service acknowledges "valid for small shops"), but should be tracked as follow-up work before the customer base grows. W3 is low-risk but should be addressed to prevent future type drift.

---

## Branch 2: `feat/admin-hardening-c3`

### Summary
Adds JWT audience (`aud`) claim enforcement. New `JwtAudienceGuard` (global APP_GUARD) validates that JWTs targeting admin endpoints carry `aud='admin'`, and shop-customer JWTs carry `aud='shop'`. Also adds `AdminPrefixMiddleware` that strips `/api/admin/` → `/api/` so the frontend can namespace admin API calls without controller changes. Changes the web app's default API base URL from `/api` to `/api/admin`.

**Files changed**: 8 files, +373 / -4 lines  
**Commits**: 5

### Issues

#### Warning (should fix / plan before merge)

**W1 — Breaking existing sessions on deployment**  
`auth.service.ts` now issues `aud: 'admin'` on all new tokens. `JwtAudienceGuard` throws `ForbiddenException` when `req.user.aud !== 'admin'` on any non-public `/api/*` path. Tokens issued before deployment (without `aud`) will trigger 403 on every request until the user refreshes. Since `/api/auth/refresh` and `/api/auth/login` are in `PUBLIC_PATHS`, token refresh works, but users mid-session will see 403 errors until their access token expires (~15 min depending on TTL). Deployment should be coordinated with low-traffic window or a force-logout mechanism.

**W2 — Frontend API base URL change affects all environments**  
`apps/web/src/lib/env.ts` changes the default API URL from `/api` to `/api/admin`. Vite proxies `/api/*` → `localhost:3000` (confirmed in `vite.config.ts`), so dev works. Production nginx/CDN proxies also forward `/api/*` to the backend, so `/api/admin/*` is covered. However, any external scripts, CI tests, or API client code that hardcode `/api/` directly will no longer route through the audience-prefixed path. The `JwtAudienceGuard` still validates `aud` regardless of URL prefix, so this is a developer-experience concern rather than a security hole.

**W3 — Misleading test description** (`apps/api/src/modules/auth/guards/jwt-audience.guard.spec.ts:44`)  
Test at line 44 is named `"throws ForbiddenException when JWT has no aud claim but decorator requires one"` but asserts `toBe(true)`. The comment inside clarifies the correct behaviour (req.user undefined → defer to JwtAuthGuard), but the description will confuse future readers. Should be renamed to `"defers to JwtAuthGuard when no user on decorator-guarded endpoint"`.

#### Info

**I1 — `scope: 'admin:full'` payload field is currently unused**  
`auth.service.ts` adds `scope: 'admin:full'` to JWT payloads but `JwtAudienceGuard` only reads `aud`. The `scope` field is inert for now. Fine as forward-compatible scaffolding, but note it adds 16 bytes to every token.

**I2 — No `@RequireAudience` usage yet**  
The decorator-based override mode in `JwtAudienceGuard` is fully implemented and tested but not applied to any controller yet. The guard currently relies entirely on path-based inference. This is expected for Batch C3 — decorator usage will come in C4+.

### Recommendation: **REVIEW**

No security critical issues. W1 requires a deployment plan: document in ops runbook that existing sessions will receive 403s for up to one access-token TTL after rollout. Consider adding a forced-refresh hint (e.g., `X-Token-Version` response header) as a follow-on.

## Branch 3: `refactor/contract-create-unify-docs`

### Summary
Removes Step 4 (document upload) from the contract creation wizard. The flow changes from 4 steps to 3 steps: Product → Customer → Plan + Confirm. Document upload is deferred to the contract detail page. Removes `DocumentUploadStep` component, `useDocumentUpload` hook, `PendingDoc` type, and `DOCUMENT_TYPES` constant. Simplifies submit to single "สร้างสัญญา" button (removes "บันทึกร่าง" and "สร้าง + ส่งตรวจสอบ" split buttons).

**Files changed**: 9 files, +80 / -600 lines  
**Commits**: 1

### Issues

#### Warning (business sign-off required)

**W1 — Required KYC documents no longer collected at contract creation**  
The removed step previously collected: สำเนาบัตรประชาชน (required), KYC selfie (required), IMEI photo (required), Down payment receipt (required), PDPA consent (required). These are now deferred to the contract detail page. Thai financial regulation and internal KYC policy require document collection before or at the time of contract activation. Merging this change without confirming with the business/legal owner that "document upload at detail page" satisfies compliance is a risk. The toast message `"อัปโหลดเอกสารที่หน้ารายละเอียดสัญญา"` informs staff, but enforcement (blocking activation until docs are present) is not added in this PR.

**W2 — "Submit for Review" auto-flow removed without replacement**  
The `submitForReview` flag called `POST /contracts/:id/submit-review` automatically. This is now gone — contracts are created in their initial state with no automatic review request. Staff must navigate to the detail page and manually trigger review. If any downstream workflow (contract approval queue, notifications) depends on the submit-review call happening at creation time, this is a silent regression.

**W3 — OCR scan no longer auto-populates document attachment**  
`useOcrFlow` previously auto-added the scanned ID card image to `pendingDocs` on OCR success. This is removed. OCR still works (fills customer form fields) but the image itself is discarded from the upload queue. Users lose the convenience of having the ID card scan automatically attached to the contract.

#### Info

**I1 — Clean removal of circular dependency workaround**  
The `setPendingDocsRef` `useRef` hack that broke the `useOcrFlow` ↔ `useDocumentUpload` circular dependency is cleanly removed. Code quality improvement.

**I2 — Draft save button removed**  
The "บันทึกร่าง" (Save Draft) button is removed. `useDraftStorage` (auto-save to localStorage) still runs, so the draft *is* saved automatically — but the manual save button is gone. Users relying on it may be confused.

### Recommendation: **REVIEW — business owner sign-off required**

The code changes themselves are clean (W1–W3 are business/process decisions, not code defects). However, W1 is a compliance risk that must be confirmed with the business owner before merge. If the owner confirms that documents can be uploaded after creation (and the system will enforce this before contract activation), this branch is safe to merge.

| `feat/admin-hardening-c3` | 0 | 3 | 2 | **REVIEW** |

No branch is **BLOCKED** (no critical code defects). All three require review comments or business sign-off before merge:

1. **customer-tier-phase1**: N+1 query + broken pagination with tier filter — track as follow-up or fix before merge if customer base > 200.
2. **admin-hardening-c3**: Deployment coordination needed for token transition window.
3. **contract-create-unify-docs**: Business owner must confirm deferred-document compliance is acceptable.
